title: Markdown Document Export for Data Dictionary
story: As an end user, I want to export my data dictionary as a formatted markdown document, so that I can distribute a readable metrics glossary to business stakeholders or publish it to tools like Confluence.
description:
Add a markdown export option for the data dictionary. The export should produce a well-formatted document suitable for sharing with non-technical stakeholders or publishing to documentation platforms like Confluence.

### Export Modes
- **Draft** — working version for internal review
- **Published** — finalized version for distribution (see MBT-586)

### Use Case
Business users often find it easier to read a formatted document than a large spreadsheet. Markdown is a widely supported format that renders cleanly across tools.
acceptance_criteria: Data dictionary can be exported as a markdown document
Export includes all relevant dictionary fields formatted readably
Draft and Published export modes are supported
Exported markdown renders correctly in Confluence and GitHub
ux_changes: New export button or menu option for markdown document download with Draft/Published mode selection.
middleware_changes: Backend logic to transform dictionary data into structured markdown format.
comment_summary: Developer requested clarification on what information should appear in the markdown output and asked for an example.
